Part B.key_evidence: Science and reference show a very bright, saturated star at/near center (PS1 neighbor at 3.1" with srmag1=14.88, sgscore1=0.92). The difference image shows a messy dipole/ring-like residual rather than a clean PSF-like positive source. Despite ncovhist=1695 visits, there is only ndethist=1 historical detection, consistent with a one-off subtraction artifact around a bright star.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a bogus subtraction artifact caused by PSF/astrometric mismatch on a bright, likely saturated stellar source. The dipole residual in the difference, proximity to a very bright PS1 star, and large sigmapsf (~0.2 mag) at m~18 all support this.
Part B.alternative_analysis: A real stellar variable blended with the bright neighbor is possible (high sgscore1, point-like reference source), but the difference morphology is not a clean positive PSF and no prior detections exist over 1695 visits, making genuine variability less likely than an artifact.
